one_line: SF Pro family, monochrome with subtle product accents. Hero-driven full-bleed sections. One focal point per fold. Feels like apple.com, Things3, Tonal.

background.role: Pure white — Apple uses pure white correctly. The negative space carries the design.

surface.role: Pearl/off-white for product cards and section bg.

surface_dark.role: Pure black sections for product-dark presentations. Apple's signature alternating light/dark sections.

text_primary.role: Apple's official body color. Near-black, slight cool tint.

accent_blue.role: Apple's link blue. Used for links + outline CTAs only.

accent_red.role: Sparingly — Apple Maps or 'subscribe' style. Muted.

background.role: Pure black, used for product-dark hero sections.

rules: Pure black AND pure white are correct here — Apple's marketing alternates light + dark full-bleed sections, and the contrast is the design
Accents are SPARINGLY used. The 'color' in an Apple marketing page is the product imagery itself, not chrome
Single accent (blue link / red flourish) per page — Apple doesn't paint CTAs in brand colors except in very specific contexts
Sections alternate: white bg → product hero → dark bg → product detail → white bg. This rhythm IS the design

display.family: SF Pro Display, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, system-ui, sans-serif
display.rule: SF Pro Display is the system font on Apple devices and matches apple.com. Fall back to Inter for cross-platform — it's the closest match.

body.family: SF Pro Text, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, system-ui, sans-serif

scale.$rule: Base 17px — Apple uses 17px body. Headlines go BIG: hero is often 80-120px on desktop.

rules: Hero headlines are HUGE — 80-128px on desktop. Apple doesn't whisper headlines
Tight tracking on large sizes (-0.04em) — looser tracking ruins the polish
Body weight 400 — not the bolder 500 that other profiles use. Apple body is light
Pricing tables: monospace, tabular numbers, right-aligned
Section gutters: 128-200px vertical. This profile has the largest vertical rhythm of any in the pool
Full-bleed sections: 100% width, 96-128px vertical padding inside
Apple uses larger radii than most profiles — buttons can be xl (18px) or even pill-shaped for primary 'Buy' CTAs
Pill buttons are CORRECT here for primary marketing CTAs ('Buy', 'Pre-Order') — one of the few profiles where pills work
Almost no shadows. Apple uses pure surfaces and color separation, not depth tricks
Product imagery often has its OWN shadow built into the asset (rendered with realistic light)
If a shadow is needed: 0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.04) in light, 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.06) in dark

button_primary.radius: xl (18px) OR full (pill) for buy CTAs
button_primary.rule: Blue pill buttons are the Apple primary. NOT colored gradients.

button_secondary.rule: Apple's secondary is often just blue text with an arrow ('Learn more →'). No outlined button needed.

feature_card.rule: Apple's feature cards are large, often full-section. Inside: huge feature claim + product imagery.

marketing_hero.structure: Full-bleed white (or black) section, 600-800px tall on desktop. Centered: product name (small, 14px), enormous headline (5xl-6xl, weight 600), short subhead (lg, weight 400), 'Buy' button + 'Learn more →' link. Below: large product imagery, centered.
do: ONE focal point per section — never split

feature_section.structure: Alternating white and black full-bleed sections. Each section has ONE feature claim + ONE product detail (image, animation, or chart). Vertical gap between sections: 128-200px.
do: Alternate light/dark — the rhythm is the design

pricing.structure: Three columns side-by-side, each with a tier name, big price (mono, tabular), feature checklist below. Center column slightly emphasised.
do: Feature rows use ✓ in accent_blue, not text

animations.library: gsap with ScrollTrigger (scroll-driven sections are signature)
animations.philosophy: Long, smooth, choreographed. Apple uses motion to direct attention.

patterns.scroll_reveal: Each section reveals as it enters viewport — GSAP ScrollTrigger, 600-900ms with cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1).
patterns.parallax_product: Hero product image moves slowly with scroll (translateY based on scroll position).
patterns.section_pin: Some sections PIN during scroll while elements animate inside — classic Apple. Use sparingly, 1-2 per page max.
patterns.text_word_reveal: Hero copy reveals word-by-word with 50ms stagger as it enters viewport.

anti_patterns: bouncy elastic eases (wrong vibe — Apple is smooth, not playful)
spinners on demand-load images (use blur-up instead)
